通信の暗号化（SSL/TLS）： すべての通信は暗号化されているため、同じフリー: Encryption of Communication (SSL/TLS): Since all communications are encrypted, it prevents third parties on the same network, such as free Wi-Fi, from eavesdropping on your data.
チャットツールの自動圧縮による品質低下： メッセージアプリで画像や動画を送ると、: Quality Degradation Due to Auto-Compression in Chat Tools: Sending images or videos via messaging apps often causes automatic resizing and high compression, which can significantly impair the original image quality or resolution.
パスワードの伝達： パスワードは、チャットツールとは「全く別のルート」で伝えます: Conveying the Password: The password must be communicated through an "entirely different channel" than the chat tool. Specifically, this includes making a phone call, sending an SMS (short text message to a phone number) on a smartphone, or sending it to a pre-configured alternative contact email address.
設定を調整する（任意）： 必要に応じて、保持期間（例：1時間、1日など）の変更や: Adjust Settings (Optional): Change the retention period (e.g., 1 hour, 1 day, etc.) or set up ID/password protection as needed.
自動データ消去： 転送されたデータは、設定した時間が経過するとサーバー上から痕跡: Automatic Data Deletion: The transferred data is completely and automatically deleted from the server without leaving any traces once the set time has elapsed.

スマホでダウンロード： スマートフォン側のブラウザで開いたページからダウンロード: Download via Smartphone: Execute the download from the page opened in the smartphone's browser and save it.
履歴の残留： 共有相手のPCやスマートフォンのブラウザ履歴、チャットツール上の会: Lingering History: The concern that the URL remains semi-permanently in the browser history of the recipient's PC or smartphone, or in the chat tool's conversation history, allowing third parties to access it later.
リアルタイム議事録： 司会進行や書記だけでなく、参加者全員がその場で決定事項や補: Real-Time Meeting Minutes: Not just the facilitator or scribe, but all participants can simultaneously input decisions and supplemental information on the spot, completing a perfect set of meeting minutes as the meeting ends.
最高レベルの安全性を維持するために、実務では以下のように「連絡経路を切り分ける（: To maintain the highest level of security, please strictly implement "separating communication channels" in your business practice as follows.
PCで受け取る（URL入力の場合）： PCにカメラがない場合や認識しづらい場合は: Receive on PC (via URL Input): If your PC lacks a camera or has trouble recognizing codes, you can directly input the "4 to 6-digit short code (passkey)" or the dedicated short URL shown on the smartphone's upload completion screen into your PC's browser. This makes handing over the file to your PC very simple.
QRコードを表示して読み取る： アップロード完了後に画面へ表示されるQRコードを: Display and Scan QR Code: Scan the QR code displayed on the screen after the upload is complete using your smartphone's camera.

通信経路上の盗聴： カフェなどの暗号化設定が脆弱なフリーWi-Fiを利用している: Eavesdropping on the Communication Path: When using free Wi-Fi with weak encryption settings in cafes, there is a risk that the URL may be intercepted via packet capturing (eavesdropping).
退職者や外部パートナーの権限管理不備： プロジェクトが終了したり、メンバーが退職: Inadequate Permission Management for Retirees or External Partners: Leaving old sharing links active after a project ends or members leave the company, allowing former members to access data at any time.

スキャンと閲覧： 参加者は着席時や受付時に自身のスマートフォンまたはタブレットの: Scan and View: Participants can open the materials on their own devices instantly just by scanning with their smartphone or tablet camera upon seating or reception. Installing special apps or registering for authentication is completely unnecessary.
ブレインストーミング： アイデア出しのディスカッションにおいて、各自のPCから付: Brainstorming: In discussion sessions for generating ideas, each person can write text from their own PC like sticky notes, visualizing them in real-time for everyone.
メール添付の容量上限： 一般的なビジネスメールでは添付ファイルは20MB〜25M: Email Attachment Capacity Limit: In general business emails, the limit for attached files is 20MB to 25MB, and if it exceeds that, it cannot be sent due to an error.
リンクの流出と徘徊（スクレイピング）： 放置された共有URLが、何らかの理由で第: URL Leakage and Scraping: The risk that a sharing URL left active gets into the hands of a third party for some reason, or is crawled/detected by search engine crawlers or malicious programs (scrapers), exposing confidential data.
クラウドストレージの容量圧迫： Google DriveやDropbox、One: Cloud Storage Capacity Strain: Free tiers of Google Drive, Dropbox, OneDrive, etc. quickly reach their limits, requiring you to regularly organize past files or upgrade to a paid plan.
保存する： ページ上に表示されたファイル名とサイズを確認し、「ダウンロード」ボタ: Save: Check the file name and size displayed on the page and tap the "Download" button. Once processing is complete, the file will be saved to your device.
ファイルをアップロードする際、送信者は任意のパスワードを設定できます。そして、シ: When uploading a file, the sender can set an arbitrary password. The system then automatically generates a "temporary ID (user ID)" dedicated to that file along with the URL for downloading. Recipients accessing the download screen cannot even access the actual file unless they enter both the combination of the "system-issued temporary ID" and the "password set by the sender".
アカウント乗っ取りの二次被害： チャットツールやメールのアカウントが万が一乗っ取: Secondary Damage from Account Hijacking: The risk that if a chat tool or email account is hijacked, all confidential files spanning the past several years could be downloaded from "valid sharing links" remaining in the past logs.
相手にも負担をかけない：受け取る側にもアカウント作成を求めないサービスが多いため: No burden on the recipient: Since many services do not require the receiver to create an account, you can confidently send download URLs even to those who are not tech-savvy.

プライバシーの保護：氏名やメールアドレスといった個人情報をサービス提供側に渡す必: Privacy protection: There is no need to hand over personal information such as your name or email address to the service provider. This fundamentally reduces the risk of personal data leaks.

「データは蓄積するもの」という常識から、「必要なときだけ存在する」という考え方へ: Move beyond the idea that data should always be stored, and embrace the concept of files that only exist when you need them. Use the auto-delete feature wisely to experience smart file sharing with minimal management overhead and risk.
「ブラウザさえあれば送れる」という安心感は、ビジネスでもプライベートでも大きな武: Knowing you can share files with just a browser is a powerful advantage, both at work and in your personal life. Remove every barrier to getting started, and share large files more freely and quickly than ever before.
セキュリティを気にしすぎて業務が滞っては本末転倒です。FS!QRなら、高度な保護: Letting security concerns slow your workflow defeats the purpose. With FS!QR, anyone can use advanced protection features easily and for free. Choose the right tools and settings to build a safe, secure remote work environment.
ペーパーレス化は環境に優しいだけでなく、運営の効率化と情報のアクセシビリティ向上: Going paperless is not only good for the environment—it also improves operational efficiency and makes information more accessible. For your next event or meeting, consider using FS!QR for smart, digital document distribution.
「イベント期間中だけ閲覧可能にしたい」という場合も、FS!QRの有効期限設定が役: If you want materials accessible only during an event, FS!QR's expiration settings are the perfect solution. Access is automatically revoked after the set period, making it safe to distribute even sensitive seminar or workshop materials.
一時的な共有のためにサーバーの容量を使い続けるのは、リソースの無駄遣いでもありま: Continuously occupying server space for temporary shares is also a waste of resources. Files that exist only as long as needed, and disappear once their purpose is served, represent a more efficient and cleaner approach to the internet.
FS!QRでは、共有時に「いつまで残すか」を選択できます。会議中の1時間だけ、あ: With FS!QR, you choose exactly how long a shared file stays available—just one hour during a meeting, or several days if needed. Setting the shortest viable duration helps maintain the highest possible level of security.
FS!QRで共有されたファイルは、スマートフォンの標準カメラでQRコードを読み取: Files shared via FS!QR can be received simply by scanning the QR code with your smartphone's built-in camera. No dedicated app or account registration is required—just tap the link that appears and you are in.
PCのブラウザでFS!QRを開き、送りたいファイルをドラッグ＆ドロップします。画: Open FS!QR in your PC's browser and drag and drop the file you want to send. A QR code will appear on screen—scan it with your phone's camera and the file is saved to your phone, no complicated setup required.
イベント中に「あの資料が欲しい」と言われた際も、その場でQRコードを見せるだけで: Even when someone asks for a document in the middle of an event, simply showing the QR code handles it on the spot. Share materials alongside your business card, bundle a file with a survey link—the possibilities are endless.
セミナーや会議のたびに、参加人数分の資料を印刷してホチキス留めするのは大変な重労: Printing and stapling enough copies of materials for every seminar or meeting is a significant burden. Distributing documents digitally through FS!QR not only cuts printing costs, but also allows last-minute updates with no extra effort.
さらにセキュリティを高めるなら、共有URLをチャットで送り、パスワードは電話や別: For an extra layer of security, send the share URL via chat and communicate the password by phone or a separate channel—a technique known as channel separation. Even if one account is compromised, this prevents the file itself from being accessed.
大きなファイルを誰かに送りたいとき、相手に特定のクラウドストレージのアプリを入れ: When you need to send a large file, asking the other person to install a specific cloud storage app or create an account can feel like too much to ask. With FS!QR, both sender and receiver can handle everything right in the browser they already use.
受け取ったファイルは、信頼できる共有者からのものかを確認したうえで開きましょう。: Before opening a received file, confirm it came from a trusted source. While FS!QR encrypts your connection, staying careful about what is inside the file on the recipient's end is an essential part of safe file sharing.
通常の共有リンクは、URLを知っている人なら誰でもアクセスできてしまいます。機密: A standard share link can be accessed by anyone who knows the URL. When handling sensitive work files, always enable the password protection feature. This way, even if the URL is exposed, the contents remain completely protected.
共有したファイルは自動的に削除されるため、サーバーに余分なデータを残さず安全に運: Shared files are automatically deleted, so no unnecessary data lingers on the server. You can also configure password and ID protection as needed to prevent unauthorized access and keep your information secure.
配布方法は非常にスマートです。資料をFS!QRにアップロードし、生成されたQRコ: The distribution method is remarkably smart. Upload your materials to FS!QR, then display the generated QR code on a projector or post it at the reception desk. Attendees simply scan it with their own phone or tablet to receive the latest version instantly.
一度送ったファイルのことを、いつまでも覚えているのは難しいものです。古い共有リン: It is hard to keep track of every file you have ever sent. When old share links linger unintentionally, the risk of a third party stumbling upon them grows over time. With auto-delete, the system cleans everything up reliably even when you forget.
PCで作った資料をスマホで確認したい、あるいはスマホで撮った写真をPCに送りたい: When you want to review a PC document on your phone—or send a photo from your phone to your PC—do you find yourself hunting for a USB cable or emailing yourself? With FS!QR, you can transfer files instantly between any devices as long as you have a browser.
FS!QRを日常の授業や会議に取り入れることで、資料の準備や配布にかかる時間を大: Incorporating FS!QR into your everyday lessons and meetings can dramatically reduce the time spent preparing and distributing materials. No account or special app is required, so anyone can get started right away—experience the next generation of file sharing.
逆にスマホからPCへ送るのも簡単です。スマホでFS!QRにアクセスし、ファイルを: Sending from phone to PC is just as easy. Access FS!QR on your phone, select a file, and upload it. Then either scan the generated QR code with your PC's camera (if it has one), or type the short URL shown on screen into your PC's browser.
OSを問わず、Windows, Mac, iPhone, Androidのどれか: The power of a browser-based tool is that it works exactly the same on any platform—Windows, Mac, iPhone, or Android. There is no need to wait for specific software updates or free up storage space to install yet another app.
会議では、直前の資料の差し替えや追加が頻繁に発生します。FS!QRを活用すれば、: Last-minute document swaps and additions happen frequently in meetings. With FS!QR, simply displaying a QR code on the projector is all it takes for every participant to get the latest materials on their device. The collaborative note feature also makes real-time minute-taking easy.
ファイルだけでなく、ちょっとしたテキストURLを共有したいときにもFS!QRは便: FS!QR is useful not just for files, but also for sharing short text or URLs between devices. Upload a simple text file and it becomes a powerful cross-device clipboard. Solve those small daily friction points with a single QR code.
少人数のゼミやグループワークでは、アイデア出しや役割分担、共同発表資料の作成が必: In small seminars or group projects, brainstorming, dividing tasks, and creating joint presentation materials are all essential. FS!QR Note lets everyone write simultaneously to a shared note while working together. Photos and data can be exchanged instantly, with no cables required.
多くのクラウドサービスは「保存」を目的としていますが、FS!QRは「共有」そのも: While most cloud services are built around storage, FS!QR is focused on sharing itself. One of its defining features is auto-deletion—a mechanism that permanently removes data from the server once the set period expires. Here is why that matters.
FS!QRはアカウント不要で利用できるため、出先の共有PCや友人のデバイスとのや: Because FS!QR requires no account, it is perfect for use on a shared PC while out or on a friend's device. It leaves no browser history, and when you are done, simply closing the tab is all it takes. Transferred files are also auto-deleted after the set period, letting you finish your work without leaving a trace.
ファイルが表示されない場合は、共有の有効期限が切れていないかを確認してください。: If a file is not showing up, check whether the share link has expired. Since FS!QR is designed specifically for temporary sharing, files are automatically deleted after a set period. If the link is still within its validity window, try switching your network connection or refreshing your browser.
受け取り側の操作は次の3ステップだけです。まず共有された画面のQRコードにスマー: Receiving a file takes just three steps. First, point your smartphone camera at the QR code displayed on screen. Next, tap the notification or link that appears to open the download page. Finally, confirm the filename and tap the download button to save the file to your device.
共有者がパスワードを設定している場合は、ダウンロードページでIDとパスワードの入: If the sender has set a password, the download page will ask for both an ID and a password. Enter the details the sender communicated to you in advance to access the file. To prevent the password from reaching unintended parties, it is best to receive it verbally or through a separate channel.
FS!QRの保護機能は、単なるパスワード入力だけではありません。システムが発行す: FS!QR's protection goes beyond a simple password. Downloads require both a system-issued temporary ID and a password you choose yourself. This two-factor check raises the bar for unauthorized access significantly.
高画質な動画や大量の画像セットなど、大きなファイルを送る際は通信環境が安定してい: When sending large files such as high-resolution videos or extensive image sets, make sure your connection is stable. Once you upload a file to FS!QR, a dedicated download page is generated immediately. Share the URL with the other person, or simply show the QR code if they are nearby.
学校や大学の授業では、プリントやデジタル教材の配布が日常的に行われます。教員が黒: Distributing handouts and digital materials is a daily routine in schools and universities. When teachers display a QR code generated by FS!QR on the board or projector, students can instantly download materials to their own phones or tablets. This eliminates the burden of printing and keeps lessons running smoothly.
テレワークが普及する中で、社外から社内、あるいは自宅PCと会社支給PCの間でファ: As remote work becomes more widespread, opportunities to exchange files between outside and inside the office—or between a home PC and a company-issued device—are increasing. However, sharing through insecure channels can easily lead to data leaks. Use FS!QR's password protection feature properly to build rock-solid security.
ブラウザでファイルを送った後、そのファイルがずっとインターネット上に残り続けるの: After sending a file through a browser, the thought of it lingering on the internet indefinitely can be unsettling. FS!QR is built for temporary sharing—once the download is complete (or the expiration date arrives), data is automatically erased. It is an eco-friendly, secure approach to sharing that avoids accumulating files in the cloud.
自宅のWi-Fiにつなぐとき、「○○-G」と「○○-A」のように2つの名前（SS: When connecting to home Wi-Fi, you have likely seen two network names (SSIDs) like "Network-G" and "Network-A." These represent the distinct 2.4GHz and 5GHz frequency bands. Modern routers increasingly feature "Band Steering," a technology that unifies these two frequencies under a single name. In this article, we'll discuss the advantages and disadvantages of this feature, along with troubleshooting steps if your connection becomes unstable.
もし「同じ名前にしているのに通信がよく途切れる」「スマート家電が設定できない」と: If you experience frequent connection drops despite using the same SSID, or if you can't get smart home devices to set up, we recommend disabling band steering and splitting your 5GHz and 2.4GHz networks into separate names.
